name: FWGA (5-Tier Federated Mesh)
overview: FWGA (Federated World Graph Anchoring) implements a 5-tier device mesh (Mothership/Regional/Desktop/Mobile/EdgeSensor) with clearance-enforced synchronization, quorum-as-confidence thresholds, and governed conflict resolution. Unlike CouchDB-style replication where any node can overwrite any data, FWGA enforces CBAC clearance at the sync layer — a field operator at clearance 2 cannot overwrite an authority write at clearance 5.

howItWorks.title: Device Manifest
howItWorks.description: Each device registers with a DeviceManifest specifying its tier (Mothership, Regional, Desktop, Mobile, EdgeSensor), clearance level, and sync capabilities. The manifest determines what data the device can receive and what it can modify.

howItWorks.title: Clearance-Aware Sync
howItWorks.description: During sync, the clearance index ensures only appropriately-classified data flows to each device. A SECRET page never syncs to a device with clearance < 5. This is enforced cryptographically via RDKC — the device literally cannot derive the decryption key.

howItWorks.title: Conflict Resolution
howItWorks.description: When two devices modify the same entity, the 4-way conflict classifier determines resolution: clearance wins (higher clearance takes precedence), temporal wins (most recent for same clearance), confidence wins (higher confidence score), or queue for human review.

howItWorks.title: Quorum-as-Confidence
howItWorks.description: Instead of binary quorum (majority wins), TrustDB uses continuous confidence thresholds. If 3 of 5 devices agree with confidence > 0.8, the result confidence is the weighted average — not just 'quorum met/not met'.

comparisons.narrative: FWGA is the only federated sync protocol that combines device hierarchy, clearance-enforced data flow, cryptographic access control, and confidence-weighted consensus. Every competitor either uses flat replication (CouchDB), partition-based sync (Realm), or consensus without governance (Raft/Paxos).

competitors.approach: Multi-master document replication with revision trees
competitors.limitation: No clearance, no governance, last-write-wins conflict resolution
No device scoping, no confidence thresholds, no clearance

competitors.approach: Partition-based sync with custom conflict handlers
competitors.limitation: No CBAC, no cryptographic enforcement, partitions are manual
No confidence semantics, no governance, no device hierarchy
